commit.message:
Fix pxToDate: snap to column start in all zoom levels

Week view was using Math.floor((px/cw)*7) which computed a fractional
day within the week range rather than the start of the clicked column.
All zoom levels now derive the date from colIndex = Math.floor(px/cw),
matching the hoveredColLeft snap used for the visual column highlight.

docs: add FAQ, language interop section, and REST wrapper plan

questions across all doc surfaces:

- Standalone use without OpenClaw
- Difference from RAG
- Difference from fine-tuning (with link to full section)
- Artifact ownership and vendor access
- LLM compatibility via LLMFn adapter
- Artifact portability across LLM providers
- Artifact inspection and editing
- Retrieval performance as store grows
- Production readiness caveats

docs/design-decisions.md — new "Language support and interoperability"
section documenting: what non-TypeScript callers can do today (JSONL
direct access, subprocess), what requires TypeScript, the planned REST
API wrapper, and the longer-term Python library aspiration.

entry added to repository structure table.

commit.message:
Add deployment scripts, .env.example, and testnet Hardhat network

scripts/deploy.ts: four-step deploy script for Polkadot Hub testnet —
env checks (balance ≥ 1 DOT), XCMRustBridge deploy + deployments/testnet.json,
smoke test (directDotProduct([1,2,3],[4,5,6]) == 32), 5-run gas benchmark
writing benchmark-results-testnet.json.

scripts/deploy-ink.sh: guided shell script for cargo contract instantiate
with dry-run + actual deploy steps and SS58→AccountId32 conversion hint.

.env.example: documents all four required environment variables.

hardhat.config.ts: load dotenv/config, add polkadotHubTestnet network
(chainId 420420421, driven by env vars).

package.json: add "deploy" script and dotenv devDependency.

commit.message:
fix: remove invalid export from national page.server.ts

SvelteKit only allows specific named exports from +page.server.ts:
load, prerender, csr, ssr, trailingSlash, config, actions, entries,
or anything with a '_' prefix.

'nationalCategories' was exported, causing SvelteKit to throw
"Invalid export" on every request to /national/[category] → 500.

Fix: remove `export` keyword from nationalCategories (used only locally).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore date/time icons with proper padding-left so native picker isn't clipped

16px font-size, 44px min-height, -webkit-appearance:none, padding-left:40px
for icon space. Icons positioned on physical left (LTR side of input).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: multi-agent compatibility — Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Aider, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode, MCP

Agent adapters added:
- AGENTS.md — universal instruction file (OpenAI Codex CLI, Devin, generic agents)
- adapters/cursor/.cursorrules — Cursor and Windsurf
- adapters/cline/.clinerules — Cline, Roo-Code, Roo-Cline
- adapters/continue/config.json — Continue (VS Code / JetBrains) with slash commands
- adapters/aider/.aider.conf.yml + system_prompt.md — Aider
- adapters/codex/instructions.md — OpenAI Codex CLI and GPT-based agents
- adapters/gemini/GEMINI.md — Gemini CLI
- adapters/opencode/opencode.md — OpenCode

MCP server (works with any MCP-compatible agent):
- mcp_server/server.py — FastMCP + raw MCP SDK fallback
- Tools: detect_file_type, extract_engineering_data, parse_las_file,
         parse_drilling_report, classify_discipline, run_sanity_checks, get_skill_definition
- Resources: schema://extraction-output, schema://file-types

Python package:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use express.json verify callback to capture rawBody for HMAC

Previous approach (express.raw() as route middleware) was unreliable —
express.json() had already consumed the body stream before the route ran.

New approach:
- express.json({ verify: (req, res, buf) => req.rawBody = buf }) captures
  the raw Buffer on every request before JSON parsing
- Webhook route uses req.rawBody for HMAC and req.body for parsed order
- No more express.raw() needed; removes route ordering dependency

Fix dashboard Last Active and Recent Activity bugs

- Filters out idle notifications from activity feed

This makes the dashboard actually show real work being done.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disable API key rate limiting to prevent false 401s on reconnect

Better Auth's apiKey plugin defaults to 10 verifications per 24 hours.
Each WebSocket reconnect calls verifyApiKey(), exhausting the quota and
causing agents to get 401 errors after ~10 reconnections in a day.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: create MoqKnownSymbols per compilation instead of per operation (#1026)

## Summary

- Refactored `SetupShouldBeUsedOnlyForOverridableMembersAnalyzer`,
`RaiseEventArgumentsShouldMatchEventSignatureAnalyzer`, and
`MethodSetupShouldSpecifyReturnValueAnalyzer` to create
`MoqKnownSymbols` (and `KnownSymbols`) once per compilation via
`RegisterCompilationStartAction` instead of per operation/syntax-node
action.
- This eliminates redundant allocation of 53 `Lazy<T>` fields on every
operation action invocation.
- Follows the same pattern already used by
`AsShouldBeUsedOnlyForInterfaceAnalyzer`,
`CallbackSignatureShouldMatchMockedMethodAnalyzer`, and other analyzers
in the codebase.

Fixes #1022

## Test plan

- [x] `dotnet build` succeeds with zero warnings and zero errors
- [x] All 2892 tests pass (`dotnet test`)
- [x] Verified refactored analyzers follow the existing
`RegisterCompilationStartAction` pattern
- [x] Early return when Moq is not referenced (`IsMockReferenced()`
guard)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Moved analyzer symbol initialization to a per-compilation stage and
reduced per-invocation overhead for faster analysis.
* Cached known-symbol information to avoid repeated work, improving
performance and resource use during analysis.
* No changes to public diagnostics or external behavior; analyzer
findings remain the same.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
fix(cc-native): update stale _shared/lib-ts imports to _core/lib-ts

All 41 CC-native template files imported from `_shared/lib-ts/base/` which
no longer exists — the shared library was renamed to `_core` and restructured
into `hooks/`, `runtime/`, and `context/` subdirectories. This caused all 6
CC-native hooks to silently crash on import, making plan review, question
gates, and task validation completely non-functional.

commit.message:
Add fault spec status command and --init flag for accept

`fault spec status` shows spec progress locally — no diff, no LLM call
needed. Parses the spec file, scans for code anchors, and cross-references
the baseline to show what's implemented vs what remains.

`fault spec accept --init` bootstraps a baseline from the entire repo
without requiring the empty-tree workaround.
